text.title: Partner Swap Deal Template Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of Partner Swap Deal Template MUST enforce. Rationale + source per rule. Rules referenced by id from `content/06-decision-tree.xml`.

rule.statement: Swap partners MUST be in adjacent (not competing) specialties. Same-specialty swaps are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Same-specialty swaps collapse on every overlap; trust erodes; deals break in 6 months.

rule.statement: Spec MUST name fee-split policy (referral fee X%, finders fee flat $, or none — symbolic only). Implicit splits cause disputes.
rule.rationale: Implicit fee handling generates 60% of swap-deal disputes; explicit terms avoid it.

rule.statement: Spec MUST define what happens when both partners want the same client (first-touch wins, joint pitch, decline). Un-defined conflicts kill deals.
rule.rationale: Conflict-of-interest moments determine whether the deal survives or breaks; defined rules survive them.

rule.statement: Spec MUST name in-scope referral types (vertical, stack, budget band). Out-of-scope swaps create noise and reciprocity guilt.
rule.rationale: Generic 'send anyone' swaps generate misfit referrals; named scope filters at the source.

rule.statement: Deal MUST be audited annually (swap count, win-rate, fee-split balance). Indefinite deals without audit lose trust quietly.
rule.rationale: Audits surface drift early; renegotiations are easier than breakups.

rule.statement: If applicability preconditions are unmet (see AGENTS.md Skip If), the methodology MUST be skipped and a one-line skip-reason logged.
rule.rationale: A named skip terminal keeps decision-tree leaves resolvable and prevents silent misuse on misfit inputs.
